steps.title: Avoid solving for $n$
steps.content: The target expression is $2n-1$, so it is faster to find $2n$ directly. From $\dfrac{2n}{5}=10$, multiply both sides by $5$ to get $$2n=50.$$

steps.title: Use the target expression
steps.content: Now substitute $2n=50$ into $2n-1$: $$2n-1=50-1=49.$$ This avoids an extra step of solving for $n$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: B is correct. Choice C (‘50’) is the value of $2n$ before subtracting $1$, and choice A (‘24’) is a trap from using $n-1$ instead of $2n-1$.
